title: HTTP Client Ownership, Context, Status, Body Limits, and Cleanup · Runtime Contract: Pokedex Go Client Reuse
summary: Each command creates a new client, uses context.Background inside dependencies, ignores non-2xx status, decodes unbounded bodies, and forgets to close responses. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go client reuse increment in reusable bounded context-aware PokéAPI client. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chan…
objectives: Inject and reuse one configured http.Client or narrow transport port because transports retain connection state and support concurrent use.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Predict Pokedex Go Client Reuse · Theory Pokedex Go Client Reuse
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 9678 during a fair-use API stewardship review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove client reuse, response admission, bounded bodies, context cancellation, and cleanup; failed and canceled commands must preserve the prior admitted state; then inject one cache, waiter, goroutine, cancellation, or queue schedule and retain ownership evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject and reuse one configured http.Client or narrow transport port because transports retain connection state and support concurrent use.
